item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know what people are talking about what with 'advertisement for another skip', I just enjoyed this one a lot. I'd like to know who this is, what the treaty was, and why…
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reaching the western edge of SCP-3702-1 prior to 9:00 P.M. will also result in demanifestation.

Shouldn't they just be SCP-3702 and not -1? Otherwise, I actually found the last addendum very, very intriguing, and a good use of censorship. This left me curious in a good way. It is, however, a way that wishes to never be satisfied. Hearing that there is a concrete answer to what are behind those black bars makes me a little unsatisfied. I regret having read the name in your first discussion post, because now I will have to look it up and have more mystery dissipate.

+1 nonetheless. I enjoyed this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt nothing but apathy when I read the last addendum. I know it's connected to something you're writing but how am I supposed to care that the Foundation knows who this guy is and ceasing to support him if I have no clue on who it could be?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what's the identity of this creature?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I been through the desert and I ain't got no name,
It feels bad to be out of the rain,
In this desert… your number's your name,
Because the Foundation's there for to keep you contained

I like the mainlist title for this SCP. I also liked the article well enough. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh shit waddup, it's kath and their nit-pick-crit.
Nah I'm kidding, just running touch-ups

[…]entity's chest and back. These translate too:

Too —> To
Always double check the grammar :P

Not much else to add other than I agree with Weryl's points — in fact, I'd find something like this more tale-worthy than scip-worthy due to it's aforementioned dependence and/or reference to other scips and the like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't tend to like skips that exist solely to act as advertisements for other skips or tales. I'm very excited for the rest of the orcadian saga, but this doesn't do it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not feeling this one. It feels bland, as mlister already said, and we have plenty of more creatively eternally-punished beings. This relies too heavily on the promise of future addition and connections, it seems, and I'm not the biggest fan of "setup" pieces to begin with.

not really draft spoilers but whatever, collapsed
– hide block

Even with what I've read of your upcoming in progress stuff, I still don't make the connection between that stuff and this skip, aside from the fact that there's a Selkie there and the Mither is name-dropped.

Sorry, but -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's pleasantly horrific, but rather too dependent on your 001; I feel that it does not really work well on its own. It needs a little more depth and elaboration to draw out the horror and elucidate what is happening and why.

EDIT: Completely managed to miss the note at the top, for some reason. Not sure how that happened. Either way, switched to an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I understand the concept, and appreciate the connections to your other works, I can't say that I find the SCP itself interesting in the slightest. The concept itself has potential (even if it's a tad similar to 1440), but you decide to write it a very matter-of-fact and dry way, which doesn't appeal to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Many thanks to ARD for the inspiration behind this idea. This is a quickly written and fleshed out concept that plays into a broader plot working towards my 001, and regarding a rather large SCP that I am in the midst of constructing.

So for those of you wondering, this SCP is basically Finnfolk Hell. I won't say much more than that, because then I'll give away too much about what's coming down the line.
